pageDescription: Calculate the conservation of momentum in collisions

placeholders.velocity1: Enter initial velocity of first object
placeholders.velocity2: Enter initial velocity of second object
placeholders.finalVelocity1: Enter final velocity of first object (if known)
placeholders.finalVelocity2: Enter final velocity of second object (if known)

components.title: Momentum Components
components.description: Momentum is the product of mass and velocity (p = mv)

conservation.title: Conservation of Momentum
conservation.description: In an isolated system, total momentum remains constant

elastic.title: Elastic vs Inelastic Collisions
elastic.description: Kinetic energy is conserved in elastic collisions but not in inelastic collisions

criticalWarningsList.notDiagnostic: This calculator provides momentum calculations only
criticalWarningsList.actual: Actual momentum values may vary based on external forces and measurement accuracy

importantNotesList: This is an estimate based on ideal conditions
External forces like friction are not considered
Assumes an isolated system for conservation calculations
Use consistent units for all measurements

messages.enterInformation: Enter the mass and velocity information for momentum calculations
messages.resultMessage: Your momentum calculation results are shown below

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides momentum calculations based on standard physics formulas. Actual momentum values may vary based on external forces, measurement accuracy, and other factors not included in these calculations.

seekHelp.title: Physics Resources
seekHelp.content: For advanced physics calculations and collision analysis, consult physics textbooks or academic resources on classical mechanics and momentum conservation principles.

errors.mass1: Please enter a valid mass for object 1
errors.velocity1: Please enter a valid initial velocity for object 1
errors.mass2: Please enter a valid mass for object 2
errors.velocity2: Please enter a valid initial velocity for object 2
errors.finalVelocity1: Please enter a valid final velocity for object 1
errors.finalVelocity2: Please enter a valid final velocity for object 2

tooltips.mass1: Mass of the first object involved in the collision
tooltips.velocity1: Initial velocity of the first object before the collision
tooltips.mass2: Mass of the second object involved in the collision
tooltips.velocity2: Initial velocity of the second object before the collision
tooltips.finalVelocity1: Final velocity of the first object after the collision (if known)
tooltips.finalVelocity2: Final velocity of the second object after the collision (if known)

enter_object_properties_and_collision_ty: Enter object properties and collision type to calculate outcomes
all_collisions_conserve_momentum_m1u1_m2: All collisions conserve momentum: $$m_1u_1 + m_2u_2 = m_1v_1 + m_2v_2$$
elastic_collisions_also_conserve_kinetic:
Elastic collisions also conserve kinetic energy, while inelastic collisions convert some kinetic
                      energy to other forms.
both_momentum_and_kinetic_energy_conserv: Both momentum and kinetic energy conserved (e = 1)
coefficient_e_determines_energy_loss_0_e: Coefficient e determines energy loss (0 ≤ e ≤ 1)
solve_for_unknowns_using_momentum_conser: Solve for unknowns using momentum conservation
m_2_kg_u_4_ms_m_3_kg_u_0_ms_elastic_coll: m₁ = 2 kg, u₁ = 4 m/s, m₂ = 3 kg, u₂ = 0 m/s, Elastic collision
